title: Phase 1: Skip CLI picker, show launch modal on no-config startup
description: In cli.py, replace _first_run_picker() call with an empty SessionConfig(project=cwd.name, agents=[]) so the TUI opens with no agents. In app.py on_mount, when config.agents is empty, push LaunchAgentScreen modal. On dismiss with a result, launch the agent. On dismiss with None (cancel), exit gracefully.
design: cli.py: in _resolve_config, the no-config TUI branch returns SessionConfig(project=Path.cwd().name, agents=[]). app.py: on_mount, after existing logic, if not self.config.agents: push LaunchAgentScreen and handle result like _do_launch does.
acceptance_criteria: 1. synth with no config opens TUI and shows LaunchAgentScreen modal. 2. Selecting a harness and submitting launches the agent. 3. Cancelling the modal exits the app.

title: Remove mark_agents_restorable and fix headless shutdown
description: Remove the dead active→restorable transition during shutdown, update session expiry SQL, fix headless CLI missing shutdown_default_executor(), and update stale docstrings.
design:
4 files to change:
1. broker/lifecycle.py: Delete mark_agents_restorable() method. Update _on_acp_session_created docstring to remove mention of restorable→active transition.
2. broker/broker.py: Remove the mark_agents_restorable() call and its try/except from shutdown().
3. db.py: Update _EXPIRE_SQL from status='restorable' to status IN ('restorable','active') so old sessions are still cleaned up.
4. cli.py: In the headless _run() finally block, after broker.shutdown(), add loop.shutdown_default_executor() to prevent 5-min hang from asyncio.to_thread threads.
acceptance_criteria: 1. mark_agents_restorable method does not exist in lifecycle.py. 2. broker.shutdown() does not call mark_agents_restorable. 3. _EXPIRE_SQL matches: DELETE FROM agents WHERE status IN ('restorable','active') AND registered < ?. 4. Headless _run() calls shutdown_default_executor(). 5. All tests pass (uv run pytest -q --tb=short --no-header -rF).
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

title: Phase 4: Reactive cleanup + speedups verification + replay scroll fix
description: Spec items 9, 11, and the replay scroll fix from item 2. Item 9: Convert ActivityBar.active in gradient_bar.py to reactive(toggle_class=...) — eliminate watch_active watcher. Add has_permission reactive with toggle_class='tile-permission' to AgentTile. Item 11: Verify textual-speedups is active at runtime — add a check in cli.py or app.py entry point that logs a warning if speedups aren't loaded. Item 2 (partial): Replace set_timer(0.2, scroll_end) in app.py select_agent with double call_after_refresh or rely on anchor() (if Phase 1 anchor is sufficient, this becomes a no-op removal of the timer).
design: These are small, independent cleanups. Item 9 retargets the spec from agent_list.py to gradient_bar.py (ActivityBar.active) and adds a new reactive to AgentTile for tile-permission. Item 11 is a one-line runtime check. The replay scroll fix depends on Phase 1 (anchor) and Phase 2 (select_agent changes).
acceptance_criteria: 1. ActivityBar has no watch_active — uses reactive(toggle_class=...) instead. 2. AgentTile.has_permission reactive with toggle_class='tile-permission' replaces imperative add_class/remove_class. 3. Runtime warning if textual-speedups not active. 4. No set_timer(0.2, ...) for scroll in select_agent. 5. Tool gate passes.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged. Items 9,11,scroll timer complete.

title: Phase 3: ToolCallBlock optimizations (Lazy, RichLog, mount_compose)
description: Spec items 7, 8, 10. All touch tool_call.py. Item 7: Wrap heavy children (raw input, DiffView) in Lazy() in compose(). For DiffView, call prepare() eagerly before yielding Lazy(dv). Item 8: Replace nested VerticalScroll+Label for shell output with RichLog(max_lines=2000). Item 10: Use mount_compose() instead of individual await mount(w) loops in update_content().
design: These are independent of the conversation/app layer changes. Can run in parallel with Phase 2. DiffView.prepare() must be called before yielding Lazy(dv) — this means compose() cannot be a simple generator for DiffView items; use on_mount or a post-compose hook to call prepare() then mount. Alternative: skip Lazy for DiffView, apply only to raw input widgets. RichLog replaces the VerticalScroll(Label(...)) pattern for raw output.
acceptance_criteria: 1. compose() wraps raw_input widgets in Lazy(). 2. DiffView either wrapped in Lazy with prepare() called eagerly, or documented reason for skipping. 3. Raw output uses RichLog(max_lines=2000) instead of VerticalScroll+Label. 4. update_content() uses mount_compose() where applicable. 5. Tool gate passes.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged. Items 7,8,10 complete.

description: Spec items 3, 5, 12. All touch app.py and the tile↔feed coordination path. Item 3: Add self._tiles dict to SynthApp, cache AgentTile refs on creation, use O(1) lookup instead of query_one() in hot paths. Item 5: Add Signal on ConversationFeed for streaming state, subscribe from AgentTile for instant activity bar updates. Item 12: Fix double-click bug — replace raw mount() with ContentSwitcher.add_content(set_current=False) for both feed mount AND MCP messages panel mount.

Phase 1 must complete first since it modifies ConversationFeed. The _tiles cache is populated in add_agent_tile/select_agent and consumed in event handlers. The Signal replaces the broker→app→DOM path for streaming state only — other state updates still go through the normal path.
acceptance_criteria: 1. self._tiles dict exists, populated on tile creation, used in all AgentTile lookups (zero query_one for tiles in hot paths). 2. ConversationFeed has streaming_signal, AgentTile subscribes. 3. select_agent uses add_content() not raw mount(). 4. MCP panel uses add_content() not raw mount(). 5. Tool gate passes.

title: Phase 1: Stream layout + anchor + prune turns (conversation layer)
description: Spec items 1, 2, 4, 6. All touch the conversation/scroll layer and are tightly coupled. Item 1: Add layout: stream to .conv-scroll in app.tcss (note: also has align-vertical: bottom — test interaction). Item 2: Replace _follow tracking with anchor() in conversation.py — remove _follow, _is_at_bottom(), _scroll_to_bottom(), update on_scroll. Add anchor() call in on_mount. Item 4: Prune detached turns from _turns at top of _update_turn_visibility. Item 6: Use widget.batch() for multi-step mount/remove in conversation.py where applicable.
design: These 4 items share the ConversationFeed scroll container. Stream layout changes how children are laid out; anchor() changes how scroll position is managed; turn pruning fixes stale refs; batch() batches mount/remove. All must be tested together since they interact. Key: anchor() is on Widget base class. Check if align-vertical: bottom is compatible with stream layout — if not, remove it (anchor handles bottom-pinning).
acceptance_criteria: 1. .conv-scroll has layout: stream in app.tcss. 2. ConversationFeed has no _follow, _is_at_bottom, _scroll_to_bottom. Uses anchor() instead. 3. _update_turn_visibility prunes detached turns. 4. Multi-step mount/remove uses batch(). 5. Existing conversation tests pass. 6. Tool gate passes.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged. Items 1,2,4,6 complete.

title: Phase 3: Viewport-aware turn visibility
description: Even after batch replay, a 200-turn conversation has ~5000 widgets all participating in layout. Textual's spatial map handles rendering efficiently (only visible widgets are painted), but CSS resolution and layout arrangement still scale with total widget count. This causes sluggish scrolling and eventual visual artifacts as the tree grows.
design: Add viewport-aware visibility management to ConversationFeed: (1) Track mounted TurnContainers in an ordered list. (2) On scroll events, determine which turns are in or near the viewport (buffer of ~2 screens above/below). (3) Set display='none' on turns outside this window — removes them from layout entirely while keeping them in the DOM. (4) When user scrolls and previously-hidden turns enter the buffer zone, set display='block' to restore them. (5) Use a debounce timer to avoid thrashing on rapid scroll. (6) CRITICAL: The current active turn (self._current_turn) is ALWAYS exempt from display=none, regardless of viewport position. This ensures MarkdownStream content renders correctly even when the user has scrolled up while the agent is streaming.
acceptance_criteria: 1. TurnContainers outside the viewport buffer have display='none'. 2. Scrolling into hidden turns restores their display to 'block'. 3. After scrolling to the middle of a 200-turn conversation, the number of TurnContainers with display != 'none' is at most (visible_turns + 2*buffer_screens), verifiable by querying the DOM. 4. The current active turn (self._current_turn) always has display='block' regardless of scroll position. 5. All content is accessible by scrolling — nothing is permanently hidden. 6. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

title: Phase 2: Batch replay and mount optimization
description: Session restore replays hundreds of journal events via _replay_event(), each calling mount() individually. Each mount triggers CSS resolution + layout pass. For 200 turns this means 200+ sequential layout passes. Additionally, consecutive MessageChunkReceived events during replay stream character-by-character into MarkdownStream, which is designed for live streaming but wasteful during replay.
design: Three changes in app.py and conversation.py: (1) Wrap the event buffer drain loop in select_agent() with self.app.batch_update() to suppress intermediate repaints. (2) Pre-process journal event buffers before replay: merge consecutive MessageChunkReceived events for the same agent into a single event with concatenated chunks, so replay creates one Markdown widget per message instead of streaming. (3) In ConversationFeed, add a replay_events() method that accepts a list of events and uses mount_all() to batch-mount widgets instead of mounting them one at a time. The method should create all widget instances first, then mount them in a single call.
acceptance_criteria: 1. select_agent() wraps event buffer drain in a single batch_update() context manager. 2. Consecutive MessageChunkReceived events in the journal buffer are coalesced into single events with concatenated chunks before replay. 3. ConversationFeed has a batch replay path that creates widgets first, then mounts via mount_all or equivalent batched call. 4. Read-only conversation widgets (AgentMessage, ToolCallBlock, ThoughtBlock, PromptBubble, TurnContainer) set can_focus=False. 5. Restored conversation looks identical to live conversation. 6. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [contract-critic] STATUS: APPROVED — Round 2. All three sub-steps cover the acceptance criteria. batch_update() defers layout for batch mount effect. Coalescing covers MCR and ATR with explicit test cases.

title: Phase 1: Fix hatch CSS background bleeding
description: Remove redundant hatch CSS from inner containers. Currently hatch: right $panel 60% is applied at 8 nesting levels (#right, ContentSwitcher > *, .right-panel, ConversationFeed, .conv-scroll, ThreadDetail, #thread-detail-scroll, .input-bar). When child widgets have height:auto and layout is recalculating, parent hatch shows through before child backgrounds paint — this is the visual glitch.
design: Remove redundant hatch from inner containers. Keep hatch only on the two leaf scroll containers: .conv-scroll and #thread-detail-scroll. Remove from ALL other selectors: #right, ContentSwitcher > *, .right-panel, ConversationFeed, .input-bar, #thread-list, ThreadDetail, #msg-title-dock. The sidebar selectors (#sidebar-label-dock, #sidebar-buttons-dock, #agent-list) already have hatch commented out — leave them as-is.
acceptance_criteria: 1. hatch CSS appears only on .conv-scroll and #thread-detail-scroll selectors. 2. All other selectors that previously had hatch have it removed (not commented out). 3. Visual appearance is unchanged in normal use — hatch pattern still visible in conversation and message detail scroll areas. 4. No background bleeding through conversation bubbles.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [contract-critic] STATUS: APPROVED — Single sub-step decomposition verified against all 7 criteria. One atomic CSS edit removing hatch from 8 non-leaf selectors, keeping only .conv-scroll and #thread-detail-scroll.

title: Phase 0: Fix dot validation in handle_launch_command
description: handle_launch_command in lifecycle.py:295 uses regex ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$ which rejects dots. The AgentConfig model validator (agent.py:121) uses ^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]*$ which allows dots. The previous fix (dc96006) updated the model but missed lifecycle.py. Agents with dots in their IDs fail to launch via MCP launch_agent tool.
design: One-line fix: change lifecycle.py:295 regex from r'^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*$' to r'^[a-zA-Z0-9][a-zA-Z0-9_.\-]*$' to match the model validator.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Agent IDs with dots (e.g. builder-yocn.1) are accepted by handle_launch_command. 2. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: Fixed: lifecycle.py regex now matches agent.py model validator, allowing dots in agent IDs

title: Phase 3: Clean up terminated agent state (memory leak)
description: When an agent is terminated, clean up its accumulated state: remove ConversationFeed from ContentSwitcher DOM, remove entries from _panels, _event_buffers, _dynamic_agents. If the terminated agent was selected, switch to the next live agent.
design:
Changes in app.py on_broker_event_message for AgentStateChanged(TERMINATED):
1. After removing the tile, also remove the feed from _panels and unmount it from ContentSwitcher (if it exists).
2. Clean up _event_buffers[agent_id].
3. Clean up _dynamic_agents[agent_id].
4. If the terminated agent is selected_agent, auto-switch to the next live agent (reuse action_next_agent logic). If no live agents, clear selected_agent.
Depends on Phase 2 because the auto-switch logic needs the fixed action_next_agent that skips terminated agents.
acceptance_criteria: 1. After agent termination, _panels no longer contains the agent. 2. ConversationFeed widget is removed from DOM. 3. If terminated agent was selected, view switches to next live agent. 4. _event_buffers entry is cleaned up. 5. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

title: Phase 2: Fix TUI crash vectors and agent switching
description: Fix ContentSwitcher NoMatches crash when switching to agents whose feeds don't exist. Fix action_next_agent to skip terminated agents and include dynamic agents. Guard all ContentSwitcher.current assignments.
design:
Changes in app.py:
1. In watch_selected_agent(), guard ContentSwitcher.current assignment — if the feed ID doesn't exist in the switcher, call select_agent() to create it first (select_agent always creates the feed before setting the reactive).
2. In show_messages() closing path, guard the ContentSwitcher.current assignment similarly.
3. In action_next_agent(), build the ID list from _agent_states (all known agents, not just config), filter out TERMINATED agents. If no live agents, no-op.
4. In select_agent(), ensure the feed is always created and mounted BEFORE ContentSwitcher.current is set (it already does this, but verify the watch_selected_agent path).
acceptance_criteria: 1. Tab cycling skips terminated agents. 2. Tab cycling includes dynamic agents. 3. No NoMatches crash when switching agents. 4. show_messages close returns to a valid agent. 5. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

Revised to 2.5: defensive early return is correct since watch_selected_agent is sync and cannot await select_agent(). (2) Sub-step 2.3 diverged — revised to 2.6: show_messages closing path now delegates to select_agent() instead of raw switcher.current. (3) Coverage gap for 'all tests pass' — covered by mandatory tool gate in Phase B.

title: Phase 1: Fix shutdown ordering and crash resilience
description: Fix broker.shutdown() so sessions are always marked restorable, even on crash/partial-shutdown. Fix list_restorable_sessions to also recover orphaned 'active' agents. Fix on_unmount to clean up message_bus.
design:
Three changes in broker.py and one in app.py:
1. Move mark_agents_restorable() to be the FIRST thing in broker.shutdown(), before lifecycle.shutdown(). This ensures agents are marked restorable even if terminate calls fail.
2. In list_restorable_sessions(), change WHERE status='restorable' to WHERE status IN ('restorable','active'). Orphaned 'active' agents (from SIGKILL/crash where shutdown never ran) become visible to restore.
3. In app.on_unmount finally block, also call message_bus.stop() to close the delivery loop aiosqlite connection. Use the same try/except/pass pattern as close_db().
4. In restore_session(), update the query to also match status='active' (consistent with list_restorable_sessions change).
acceptance_criteria: 1. After normal quit, agents table shows status='restorable' for previously-running agents. 2. After simulated crash (mark_agents_restorable skipped), list_restorable_sessions still returns the session. 3. on_unmount closes message_bus even if broker.shutdown() raises. 4. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented, reviewed, and merged

title:
test_app.py: Tests for empty-agents first-run flow

Two tests in tests/ui/test_app.py:
1. When SynthApp mounts with empty agents, LaunchAgentScreen is pushed (run_test, check screen stack)
2. When the modal is cancelled (returns None) in the empty-agents flow, self.exit() is called

Uses existing _make_broker/_make_config helpers. Test 2 uses direct method call with mocked push_screen_wait.
acceptance_criteria: Both tests exist and pass: (1) empty agents → LaunchAgentScreen in screen stack, (2) cancel on empty-agents first-run → app exit.
close_reason: Two tests added and passing: (1) test_empty_agents_pushes_launch_screen_on_mount verifies LaunchAgentScreen in screen stack via run_test, (2) test_first_run_cancel_exits_app verifies _do_launch returns False and exit is called.

title:
app.py: Push LaunchAgentScreen on mount when config.agents is empty

In on_mount(), add a branch for the non-restore, empty-agents case:
- Start the broker consumer worker
- Use a @work-decorated method (_do_first_run) that calls _do_launch()
- If _do_launch returns without launching (push_screen_wait returned None), call self.exit()

Reuses existing _do_launch directly — no logic duplication. The only new code is the exit-on-None wrapper.
acceptance_criteria: When SynthApp mounts with config.agents==[] (non-restore), LaunchAgentScreen is pushed. Submitting launches the agent via _do_launch. Cancelling (None result) calls self.exit().
close_reason: Added empty-agents branch in on_mount that calls _do_first_run worker. _do_first_run calls _do_launch (reuse) and exits on cancel. _do_launch now returns bool.

title:

In _resolve_config(), step 4 (no-config, TUI mode), replace the call to _first_run_picker() with:
  return SessionConfig(project=Path.cwd().name, agents=[])

_first_run_picker remains for headless error path reference but is no longer called from the TUI path.
acceptance_criteria: When _resolve_config is called with no harness, no config_path, no discovered config, and headless=False, it returns a SessionConfig with project=cwd.name and agents=[]
close_reason: Replaced _first_run_picker() call with SessionConfig(project=Path.cwd().name, agents=[]) at line 247 of cli.py

title: Research: ui-perf-optimization
description: Dispatch researchers. Post merged findings as comments on molecule root.

title: spec-driven
description: UI performance optimizations for the Textual TUI. Long sessions with many agents suffer from O(N) layout costs, manual scroll tracking, per-event tree traversals, and unbounded turn lists. This work applies 12 targeted fixes from synth-acp-perf-spec.md to make the TUI scale to long sessions with many concurrent agents.
design:
4-phase DAG implementing all 12 spec items from synth-acp-perf-spec.md.

Phase 1 (conversation layer): Items 1,2,4,6 — stream layout, anchor(), turn pruning, batch(). Foundation that all other phases build on since it changes ConversationFeed's scroll and layout model.

Phase 2 (app layer): Items 3,5,12 — cached tile refs, Signal coordination, double-click fix. Depends on Phase 1 because it adds Signal to ConversationFeed and modifies select_agent which interacts with anchor().

Phase 3 (tool_call): Items 7,8,10 — Lazy, RichLog, mount_compose. Independent of Phases 1-2, runs in parallel with Phase 2. Only touches tool_call.py.

Phase 4 (cleanup): Items 9,11, replay scroll fix — reactive toggle_class, speedups check, timer removal. Depends on Phases 1+2 since it removes the scroll timer that Phase 1 makes unnecessary and touches files modified by Phase 2.

Key corrections from spec:
- Item 9 retargeted from agent_list.py to gradient_bar.py (ActivityBar.active) + new AgentTile.has_permission reactive
- Item 12 expanded to also fix MCP panel mount (same raw mount() bug)
- Item 6 (mount_all → mount_compose) corrected: actual code uses individual await mount(w), not mount_all
- DiffView.prepare() must be called eagerly before Lazy wrapping

Conventions: Pydantic v2 frozen models, from __future__ import annotations, Google docstrings.
acceptance_criteria: All 12 spec items implemented. Tool gate passes. No regressions in existing tests.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [research] Textual API validation: All 9 API claims CONFIRMED (stream layout, anchor(), Signal, batch(), Lazy, add_content(), mount_compose(), reactive toggle_class, textual-speedups). Minor: anchor() is on Widget base class not ScrollableContainer specifically. Codebase validation: 7 CONFIRMED, 2 PARTIALLY CONFIRMED, 1 REFUTED. Refuted: claim 9 targets agent_list.py but it has zero reactives — actual candidate is ActivityBar.active in gradient_bar.py. Discovered: MCP panel mount has same raw mount() bug. DiffView.prepare() called before mounting affects Lazy strategy.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [interview] Q1: Is idle/busy/awaiting text reactive? A: No, it's imperative via update_state(). Retarget claim 9 to gradient_bar.py ActivityBar.active + add tile-permission as reactive toggle_class on AgentTile. Q2: Fix MCP panel mount too? A: Yes. Q3: DiffView.prepare() + Lazy? A: Planner's judgment — call prepare() eagerly, defer only the mount. Q4: All 12 items? A: Yes, implement all.

Non-mergeable types pass through unchanged. Creates new frozen Pydantic instances for merged events.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [interview] Q1: How long are conversations that trigger glitches? A: Hundreds of turns, 400-500k tokens. Q2: Priority — restore speed vs live rendering? A: Both equally important. Q3: Open to collapsed history UX? A: Want full history, but lazy replay — only render when content is visible. Q4: Accept static snapshot for restore? A: No — restored sessions must look identical to live sessions.

title: Review plan: ui-perf-optimization
description: Dispatch code-plan-critic. Verify DAG completeness, criterion testability, and file scope disjointness.
close_reason: Plan approved round 2

title: UI Performance Optimization for Long Conversations
description: Optimize the Textual TUI conversation feed for long sessions (hundreds of turns, 400-500k tokens). Two problems: (1) visual glitches — background bleeding through conversation bubbles as widget tree grows, (2) slow session restore — replaying hundreds of journal events with individual mount() calls. Goal: lazy/virtualized rendering so only visible content pays rendering cost, with full interactive fidelity (DiffViews, collapsible tool calls, etc.) preserved. Restored sessions must look identical to live sessions.
design: Three-phase approach targeting the two root causes: (1) Visual glitches from redundant hatch CSS at 8 nesting levels — fix by applying hatch only at .conv-scroll. (2) Slow restore from sequential mount() calls with per-widget layout passes — fix by batch_update wrapping + journal event coalescing + mount_all. (3) Unbounded widget tree growth — fix by toggling display:none on off-screen TurnContainers, keeping layout cost proportional to visible content. Phases 1 and 2 are independent. Phase 3 depends on Phase 2. Codebase conventions: Pydantic v2 frozen models, async-first, from __future__ import annotations in all files.
acceptance_criteria: 1. No background bleeding through conversation bubbles in long sessions. 2. Session restore of 200+ turns completes in under 5 seconds (vs current unbounded time). 3. Scrolling remains smooth with 200+ turns mounted. 4. Restored sessions look identical to live sessions. 5. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: All 3 phases complete

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [research] Key findings: (1) ~25 widgets/turn, 200 turns = ~5000 widgets. (2) CSS resolution is the bottleneck, not asyncio tasks. (3) Zero batch_update usage anywhere. (4) batch_update is sync ctx mgr, Widget.batch() is async version. (5) mount_all passes all widgets in single call avoiding per-widget layout. (6) Hatch CSS on 8 nested selectors causes background bleeding. (7) display:none removes widget from layout entirely. (8) Reveal mounts one-per-frame (too slow for 200 turns). (9) DataTable Line API is for homogeneous content, not suitable directly. (10) can_focus=False is minor optimization.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text: [plan-critic round 2] APPROVED — all 5 findings resolved

title: spec-driven
description: Fix shutdown/crash resilience: (1) broker.shutdown() marks agents restorable AFTER terminate() already set them to inactive, so sessions are never restorable; (2) crash exits skip shutdown entirely, leaving agents 'active' forever; (3) unclosed aiosqlite connections hang the process after TUI exit. Key constraint: 'inactive' agents (terminated via terminate_agent tool) must NOT become restorable — only agents that were running when shutdown began.
design: Three interrelated bugs in the shutdown path. The fix must reorder shutdown operations so mark_agents_restorable runs before lifecycle.shutdown terminates agents, and ensure all aiosqlite connections are closed even on crash paths. Must be extremely careful with async DB connections per AGENTS.md guidelines.
acceptance_criteria: 1. Normal quit marks running agents as restorable in DB. 2. Agents terminated via terminate_agent tool remain inactive. 3. No process hang after TUI exit (clean or crash). 4. All existing tests pass.
close_reason: All phases implemented, reviewed, and merged. Feature complete.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text:
[research-shutdown] KEY FINDINGS:
1. Normal shutdown path is CORRECT: lifecycle.shutdown() calls session.terminate() (ACPSession method, no DB write), NOT lifecycle.terminate() (which writes inactive). So DB status stays 'active' and mark_agents_restorable() matches.
2. BUG: Crash/partial-shutdown path — if broker.shutdown() never completes, agents stay 'active' forever. list_restorable_sessions queries WHERE status='restorable', so orphaned 'active' agents are invisible to restore.
3. BUG: on_unmount finally block calls lifecycle.close_db() but NOT message_bus.stop(). If broker.shutdown() raises before reaching message_bus.stop(), the delivery loop aiosqlite connection leaks → non-daemon thread → process hang.
4. Three aiosqlite connections in main process: lifecycle._db (double-close safety net, low risk), message_bus._delivery_db (MEDIUM risk — no cleanup if stop() skipped), mcp server (subprocess, no risk).
5. permissions.py uses sync sqlite3 open-close-per-call — no concern.
6. Zero test coverage for mark_agents_restorable, full shutdown→restore cycle, or crash paths.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text:
[research-tui-crash] KEY FINDINGS:
CRASH VECTOR 1 (CRITICAL): ContentSwitcher.watch_current() raises NoMatches when setting current to non-existent child ID. watch_selected_agent() at app.py:536 does this with NO guard. If a feed was never created for an agent, this crashes the app. Textual's _handle_exception always exits the app.
CRASH VECTOR 2: show_messages() closing path sets ContentSwitcher.current for selected_agent that may never have had a feed created.
CRASH VECTOR 3: action_next_agent() only cycles config agents, can select terminated agents.
CRASH VECTOR 5: _panels dict never cleaned up on termination — feeds persist forever in ContentSwitcher DOM. Memory leak in long sessions.
CONFIRMED: Textual's _handle_exception always exits the app (calls panic/fatal_error). on_unmount DOES fire during this exit path, so broker.shutdown() will be called — but may not complete if DB operations fail.
ROOT CAUSE CHAIN: Many agents → terminated agents accumulate → Tab cycles to terminated agent whose feed was never created → ContentSwitcher.current set to non-existent ID → NoMatches → app crash → on_unmount fires → broker.shutdown() may not complete → agents stay active in DB → not restorable + aiosqlite leak → process hang.

comments.author: Derek Dardzinski
comments.text:
[interview] Q1: Keep terminated agent feeds in DOM? A: No, remove them. Tile already gets removed, user can't scroll back anyway unless it was focused. Remove from DOM for performance.
Q2: Tab cycling scope? A: User asked for clarification — Tab = action_next_agent() which cycles config agents. Should include dynamic agents and skip terminated.
Q3: Crash resilience — move mark_agents_restorable early AND treat orphaned active as restorable? A: Yes, do both.
Q4: Fix memory leak too? A: Yes, clean up feeds/buffers/state for terminated agents.

value: Shutdown hang root causes: (1) unclosed aiosqlite connections create non-daemon _connection_worker_thread threads, (2) asyncio.to_thread default executor creates non-daemon asyncio_N threads with 5-min idle timeout. Both keep the process alive after event loop exits. Fix: close all aiosqlite connections AND call loop.shutdown_default_executor() in on_unmount.

When running Python code via shell commands, always use heredoc syntax (uv run python << 'EOF' ... EOF) instead of uv run python -c with quoted strings. Shell escaping of quotes, backslashes, and special characters in uv run python -c is fragile and causes recurring failures.
